pages.title: Skymu Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Skymu: what it is, its architecture, key components, and how to build it. Links to all major child sections.

pages.title: Getting Started & Build Guide
pages.purpose: Step-by-step instructions for setting up the development environment, building the solution (Yggdrasil first, then plugins, then Skymu), target framework selection (net461 vs net5.0-windows), CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions, developer tools (InterfaceSwitcher.lua, synchronize-repo.bat and more), and creating the installer.

pages.title: Licensing & Legal
pages.purpose: Explains the dual-layer AGPL-3.0 license, the Statement of Exception mechanism, the segregation of Skymu-specific code from Skype/Microsoft assets used under fair use, and the authoritative exception list.

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of how Skymu is structured: the Universal application class, the plugin system, the MiddleMan/Yggdrasil abstraction layer, and the MVVM pattern. Links to child pages for each subsystem.

pages.title: MiddleMan Plugin Interface
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the Yggdrasil (formerly MiddleMan) .NET Standard library: the ICore interface, domain model (User, Conversation, Server, ConversationItem), authentication flow, event propagation, and the ICore/IBoard(in future) extension interfaces.

pages.title: Plugin Loader & Lifecycle
pages.purpose: How Skymu discovers, loads, and disposes plugins at runtime via reflection (PluginManager.cs), how the Universal class wires plugin events (DialogTube, MessageTube, and ListTube) to the UI, and the plugin folder convention (smp.*.dll inside *.smp directories).

pages.title: MVVM & ViewModels
pages.purpose: Explains the MVVM architecture using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm: MainViewModel (conversation state, commands, events), LoginViewModel (authentication flow), IMainWindowHolder interface, and how the view layer binds to observable properties.

pages.title: User Interface
pages.purpose: Overview of the four UI themes (4-7), the WPF window hierarchy, and how the SkypeEra/Interface setting selects which UI to launch. Links to child pages for each UI subsystem.

pages.title: UI Themes: Skype 4, 5, 6 & 7
pages.purpose: Describes the four visual themes/eras of the Skymu UI. Explains the Interface setting and how themes are loaded at startup.

pages.title: Main Window & Sidebar
pages.purpose: Details the Main window (shared between Skyaeris and Pontis), sidebar tab switching (Contacts, Recents, Servers), conversation selection, resizable sidebar splitter, the Home/Chat window toggle, and the native menu bar.

pages.title: Message Rendering & Emoji
pages.purpose: Explains MessageTools: Markdown parsing via Markdig, inline emoji rendering (EmojiDictionary mapping Unicode code points to Skype emoji names), clickable mentions/channels, attachment display, and the emoji picker flyout.

pages.title: Custom Controls & UI Utilities
pages.purpose: Documents SliceControl (a magic control with animation, button stuff (text offset, icon, etc), resizable background, and more), FrozenImage (cached frozen BitmapImage helper), WPF value converters (Converters.cs), and FrameworkExtensions (Unicode rune enumeration for .NET Framework compatibility).

pages.title: Views & Dialogs
pages.purpose: Covers the secondary windows and pages: Options dialog (window frame settings), About window, Dialog (generic message box), Notification window, WindowBase base class, ErrorWindow, Updater page (GitHub release check), AddContact page, and the CallScreen page (in-call UI with mute/video controls, pill/rectangle layout modes, and call timer).

pages.title: Plugin System
pages.purpose: Overview of the four bundled plugins (Discord, Matrix, Stub, Tox) and how they implement ICore. Links to child pages for each plugin.

pages.title: Discord Plugin
pages.purpose: In-depth coverage of the Discord plugin: REST API wrapper (API.cs, ConfigManager.cs), WebSocket gateway connection (WebSocket.cs, WebSocketManager.cs), QR-code-based login via AuthSocket.cs, message parsing (MessageParser.cs), user/status management (StatusManager.cs, UserStore.cs), Protobuf settings (PreloadedUserSettings, ProtoSettings), voice call support (CallSocket.cs, CallUDP.cs - migrating the NAudio backend to Skymu itself later), and the DAVE end-to-end encryption subsystem (DaveInterop.cs, DaveSession.cs, libdave.dll, libcrypto-3-x64.dll).

pages.title: Matrix Plugin
pages.purpose: Covers the Matrix plugin: homeserver authentication (password and Beeper/passwordless), sync loop, room/DM mapping, typing indicators, and the MatrixClasses data model.

pages.title: Stub Plugin
pages.purpose: Describes the Stub plugin: a minimal ICore implementation used for development and testing, providing hardcoded fake users, messages, and conversations without any real network connection.

pages.title: Tox Plugin
pages.purpose: Documents the Tox plugin: a P2P decentralized messaging and VoIP plugin built on libtoxcore. Covers the Core class implementing both ICore and ICall, ToxCore.cs (P/Invoke bindings to libtoxcore.dll), ToxCall.cs (NAudio-based audio I/O - migrating the NAudio backend to Skymu itself later), Callbacks.cs (native callback registration), Helper.cs (utility methods), authentication via encrypted/unencrypted profile saves, and the bundled native library (libtoxcore with all the dependencies statically linked) for both x64 and x86 architectures.

pages.title: Application Services
pages.purpose: Overview of cross-cutting services used throughout the Skymu application: database, credentials, language, sounds, Windows integration, and the user-count API. Links to child pages.

pages.title: Database Manager
pages.purpose: Explains the SQLite-based DatabaseManager: schema versioning, the five tables (Accounts, Contacts, Conversations, Participants, Messages), Skype-compatible schema design, per-user per-plugin database files stored in %APPDATA%\Skymu.

pages.title: Credentials & Authentication Storage
pages.purpose: Documents the Credentials class: encrypted XML storage of SavedCredential objects using DPAPI (ProtectedData), per-plugin credential lookup, and how credentials are used to bypass manual login on startup.

pages.title: Language & Localization
pages.purpose: Covers LanguageManager: .lang file format, runtime language switching via Settings.Language, the BrandingName substitution (replacing 'Skype' with the configured brand), and the 35+ bundled language files (including Urdu, which was never in Skype).

pages.title: Windows Integration
pages.purpose: Details the Windows-specific subsystems: Tray icon management (Tray.cs) with status-mapped icons, system sounds (Sounds.cs with WAV files across four sound packs: Skymu/, Skype2/, Skype7/, and Skype8/ with Mapping.txt), OS/platform detection (OS.cs, Runtime.cs including Wine detection), DWM composition helpers, taskbar flashing (SystemControls.cs), and startup registry management.

pages.title: User Count API & SkypeHome
pages.purpose: Explains the UserCountAPI (WebSocket-based live user counter at skymu.kier.ovh), SkypeHome (WebBrowser-based home tab that loads the local page and bridges JavaScript to C# via SkypeExternalObject COM scripting and potentially the httpfe protocol in future), and the UserServer Python backend (skymu-server.py: aiohttp REST+WebSocket server managing user sessions, online counts, and ping-based timeout cleanup).

pages.title: Installer & Distribution
pages.purpose: Overview of how Skymu is packaged and distributed for Windows end-users using NSIS. Links to child pages for the script and visual assets.

pages.title: NSIS Installer Script
pages.purpose: Deep dive into SkymuSetup.nsi and SkymuBetaSetup.nsi: the Modern vs Legacy version selection page, conditional deployment from SkymuCore (net5) or SkymuFramework (net461) (both of them in AnyCPU) output directories, registry configuration (App Paths, Uninstall, IE FeatureControl), shell integration (desktop.ini, custom folder icons), and the uninstaller lifecycle.

pages.title: Installer Visual Assets
pages.purpose: Documents the NSIS branding assets: column.bmp/column.psd (MUI welcome/finish page sidebar graphic), header.bmp/header.psd (wizard header image), beta variants (column-beta.bmp, header-beta.bmp, beta.ico, installer-beta.ico), and how they are embedded and rendered by the NSIS MUI2 framework.

pages.title: Static Assets & Resources
pages.purpose: Overview of the non-code assets bundled with Skymu: emoji image packs, sound files, icons, and branding images. Links to child pages.

pages.title: Emoji Asset Pack
pages.purpose: Explains the Skype emoji image pack structure under Skymu/Emoji/: each emoji has a named directory containing default_20 (static PNG) and default_20_anim (animated PNG) variants, how EmojiDictionary maps Unicode code points to emoji names, and how MessageTools renders them inline in WPF.

pages.title: Sound Files & Icons
pages.purpose: Documents the four WAV sound packs: Skymu/ (full HQ/remastered sound set with 31 files, the default), Skype2/ (WIP classic Skype 1/2 era set), Skype7/ (original sounds), and Skype8/ (13-file Skype 8 era set with Mapping.txt mapping Skype source names to WAV targets as reference for developers). Covers the Settings.SoundPack setting and fallback logic in Sounds.cs. Also covers the Icons/ directory (status ICO files, SKYPE5.res), and the Images/ assets used in the README.md.
